meta.name: SAS-SV Smartphone Addiction Scale
meta.description: The SAS-SV is a 10-item brief scale for assessing smartphone overuse and addiction tendency.
meta.author: Kwon et al. (2013)
meta.reference: Kwon M, et al. The smartphone addiction scale: development and validation of a short version for adolescents. PLoS One. 2013;8(12):e83558.
meta.instruction: Please rate how much you agree with each statement about your smartphone use.

questions.text: Missing planned work due to smartphone use
Having a hard time concentrating in class, while doing assignments, or while working due to smartphone use
Feeling pain in the wrists or at the back of the neck while using a smartphone
Won't be able to stand not having a smartphone
Feeling impatient and fretful when I am not holding my smartphone
Having my smartphone in my mind even when I am not using it
I will never give up using my smartphone even when my daily life is already greatly affected by it
Constantly checking my smartphone so as not to miss conversations between other people on social media
Using my smartphone longer than I had intended
The people around me tell me that I use my smartphone too much

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your smartphone use is within a healthy range. This indicates that your current substance-use-related risk is low and within a healthy range.
suggestions: Continue maintaining good smartphone usage habits
Regularly check your screen time
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle
Stay informed about risks related to substance use
Maintain the ability to make autonomous choices in social situations

ranges.label: Mild Risk
ranges.description: You may have some tendency toward excessive smartphone use, but it has not yet significantly impacted your life. A mild risk level suggests it may be helpful to pay attention to your usage patterns and maintain self-awareness.
suggestions: Set time limits for smartphone use
Put your phone away during meals and before bedtime
Enable Do Not Disturb mode to focus on work or studies
Try going for a walk without your phone
Pay attention to your usage patterns and frequency

ranges.label: Moderate Risk
ranges.description: Your smartphone use may already be affecting your daily life and health. Your score suggests that substance use may already be affecting certain areas of your life. Further evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: Use your phone's built-in screen time management features
Establish phone-free zones (e.g., bedroom, dining table)
Find alternative leisure activities
Pay attention to the impact on your neck and eyes
If you feel unable to control usage, consider consulting a professional

ranges.label: High Risk
ranges.description: Your smartphone use has reached an excessive dependency level, seriously affecting daily functioning and physical/mental health. Your score indicates elevated substance-use-related risk. Speaking with a professional for personalized support and guidance is recommended.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional help
Create a detailed smartphone usage restriction plan
Ask family and friends to help monitor your usage
Consider using a basic phone during the withdrawal period
Seek professional addiction treatment as soon as possible
